section.heading: Tanks on tribal lands

subsection.heading: Strategy
subsection.chapeau: The Administrator, in coordination with Indian tribes, shall, not later than 1 year after August 8, 2005, develop and implement a strategy—

paragraph.chapeau: giving priority to releases that present the greatest threat to human health or the environment, to take necessary corrective action in response to releases from leaking underground storage tanks located wholly within the boundaries of—

subparagraph.content: any other area under the jurisdiction of an Indian tribe; and

paragraph.chapeau: to implement and enforce requirements concerning underground storage tanks located wholly within the boundaries of—

subparagraph.content: any other area under the jurisdiction of an Indian tribe.

subsection.heading: Report
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 2 years after August 8, 2005, the Administrator shall submit to Congress a report that summarizes the status of implementation and enforcement of this subchapter in areas located wholly within—

paragraph.content: the boundaries of Indian reservations; and
any other areas under the jurisdiction of an Indian tribe.

subsection.continuation: The Administrator shall make the report under this subsection available to the public.

content.p: This section does not relieve any person from any obligation or requirement under this subchapter.
Nothing in this section applies to any underground storage tank that is located in an area under the jurisdiction of a State, or that is subject to regulation by a State, as of August 8, 2005.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 89–272, title II, § 9013, as added Pub. L. 109–58, title XV, § 1529(a), Aug. 8, 2005, 119 Stat. 1101.)
